attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

<system-reminder>This memory is 18 days old. Memories are point-in-time observations, not live state — claims about code behavior or file:line citations may be outdated. Verify against current code before asserting as fact.</system-reminder>
1	---
2	name: AEM Project Overview
3	description: High-level overview of the Dell AEM learning portal project (devstore) — purpose, modules, integrations, CI/CD
4	type: project
5	originSessionId: 62767ced-1e42-4928-a161-34e6b010f8e3
6	---
7	**Project**: Dell Technologies Learning Hub (AEM Devstore)  
8	**Artifact**: devstore, version 1.0.0-SNAPSHOT  
9	**Purpose**: AEM-based education services portal for Dell Technologies training — users discover, evaluate, and register for courses (classroom, virtual, on-demand, certifications, packages).
10	
11	**Why:** Enterprise learning portal for Dell's customers/partners, integrating multiple external systems for course aggregation, search, auth, and purchases.  
12	**How to apply:** Frame all component/service work in the context of a training catalog UX with multi-currency, multi-locale, and partner-tier support.
13	
14	## Maven Module Structure
15	
16	| Module | Purpose |
17	|--------|---------|
18	| core | Java OSGi bundle — services, models, servlets, filters, REST clients, beans |
19	| ui.apps | AEM HTL components, dialogs, clientlibs (JS/CSS) |
20	| ui.config | OSGi configurations per environment |
21	| common.core | Minimal shared utility module |
22	| all | Deployment aggregator (produces devstore.all-1.0.0-SNAPSHOT.zip) |
23	
24	- `ui.frontend` module is commented out/disabled
25	- Java 1.8, AEM 6.5.17, Core WCM Components 2.21.2
26	- Node v16.17.0 / npm 8.15.0 for frontend
27	
28	## Key Integrations
29	
30	- **Coveo Search** — org IDs `dellsandbox2` (dev) / `dellprod` (prod), SearchHub `EducationHub`, faceted search + analytics
31	- **Thingworx** (OneLMS.CourseAggregatorServiceManager) — course aggregation API at `ptcappstg02.isus.emc.com:8080`
32	- **SAML 2.0** — enterprise SSO with redirect/callback/logout/response-processor servlets
33	- **PRM (Partner Relationship Management)** — Sharepoint integration for partner content
34	- **Voucher System** — training voucher generation and management
35	- **Layer7** — API gateway/security layer
36	- **Email Service** — notifications/confirmations
37	- **Tech Direct** — technical documentation API
38	
39	## CI/CD (.gitlab-ci.yml)
40	
41	- Build image: `harbor.dell.com/devops-images/debian-12/java-8-jdk:latest`
42	- Stages: compile → publish (Artifactory via HashiCorp Vault) → unit-tests / malware-scan / inclusive-lang → deploy per env → RFC → DAST
43	- Environments: DEV (auto on feature branch), STAGE/GE2 (manual), PREPROD/SIT (manual), PROD (manual + RFC approval)
44	- Target artifact: `all/target/devstore.all-1.0.0-SNAPSHOT.zip`
45	- Secrets via HashiCorp Vault
46

file.content:
---
name: AEM Project Overview
description: High-level overview of the Dell AEM learning portal project (devstore) — purpose, modules, integrations, CI/CD
type: project
originSessionId: 62767ced-1e42-4928-a161-34e6b010f8e3
---
**Project**: Dell Technologies Learning Hub (AEM Devstore)  
**Artifact**: devstore, version 1.0.0-SNAPSHOT  
**Purpose**: AEM-based education services portal for Dell Technologies training — users discover, evaluate, and register for courses (classroom, virtual, on-demand, certifications, packages).

**Why:** Enterprise learning portal for Dell's customers/partners, integrating multiple external systems for course aggregation, search, auth, and purchases.  
**How to apply:** Frame all component/service work in the context of a training catalog UX with multi-currency, multi-locale, and partner-tier support.

## Maven Module Structure

| Module | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| core | Java OSGi bundle — services, models, servlets, filters, REST clients, beans |
| ui.apps | AEM HTL components, dialogs, clientlibs (JS/CSS) |
| ui.config | OSGi configurations per environment |
| common.core | Minimal shared utility module |
| all | Deployment aggregator (produces devstore.all-1.0.0-SNAPSHOT.zip) |

- `ui.frontend` module is commented out/disabled
- Java 1.8, AEM 6.5.17, Core WCM Components 2.21.2
- Node v16.17.0 / npm 8.15.0 for frontend

## Key Integrations

- **Coveo Search** — org IDs `dellsandbox2` (dev) / `dellprod` (prod), SearchHub `EducationHub`, faceted search + analytics
- **Thingworx** (OneLMS.CourseAggregatorServiceManager) — course aggregation API at `ptcappstg02.isus.emc.com:8080`
- **SAML 2.0** — enterprise SSO with redirect/callback/logout/response-processor servlets
- **PRM (Partner Relationship Management)** — Sharepoint integration for partner content
- **Voucher System** — training voucher generation and management
- **Layer7** — API gateway/security layer
- **Email Service** — notifications/confirmations
- **Tech Direct** — technical documentation API

## CI/CD (.gitlab-ci.yml)

- Build image: `harbor.dell.com/devops-images/debian-12/java-8-jdk:latest`
- Stages: compile → publish (Artifactory via HashiCorp Vault) → unit-tests / malware-scan / inclusive-lang → deploy per env → RFC → DAST
- Environments: DEV (auto on feature branch), STAGE/GE2 (manual), PREPROD/SIT (manual), PROD (manual + RFC approval)
- Target artifact: `all/target/devstore.all-1.0.0-SNAPSHOT.zip`
- Secrets via HashiCorp Vault

content.text:
The email is not hardcoded in any component — it's in the **authored page content** stored in the JCR (the AEM repository), not in the git codebase. This is content that was typed/pasted into a Rich Text component on the page.

To fix this, you have two options:

**Option 1 — AEM Author (recommended)**
1. Go to AEM Author → navigate to `/content/emc/en-us/voucher/cdt-home/third-party-vouchers`
2. Edit the page, find the Rich Text component containing the `ProvenHelpDeskVendoreMail@Dell.com` mailto link
3. Update the email to `services.learning.support@dell.com` in both the link URL (`mailto:services.learning.support@dell.com`) and the display text if it's shown
4. Activate/replicate the page to publish

**Option 2 — CRXDE Lite**
1. Open CRXDE at `/crx/de/index.jsp`
2. Navigate to `/content/emc/en-us/voucher/cdt-home/third-party-vouchers/jcr:content`
3. Use the Search tab (Ctrl+F) to search for `ProvenHelpDeskVendoreMail` within that subtree
4. Update the property value containing the old email and save

Since this is authored content, there's no code change to make in this repository. The fix lives entirely in the AEM content repository on the author instance.

The local `cdtrequest.html` in git only has the button — it doesn't contain the email. The CRXDE version has been directly edited and is out of sync with the git repo.

To fix this properly, I need the full content of `cdtrequest.html` from CRXDE. Please do the following:

1. Open CRXDE at `/crx/de/index.jsp`
2. Navigate to `/apps/voucher/components/content/cdtrequest/cdtrequest.html`
3. Copy the full file content from the editor panel
4. Paste it here

Once I have the full CRXDE content, I'll update the email and replace the local file so it's in sync and deployable.

content.text:
The page content for `/content/emc/en-us/voucher/cdt-home/third-party-vouchers` is not in this git repo at all — it's pure authored content in the JCR. The email is stored in the `desc` property of a `cdttermsmodal` component instance on that page.

**Fix it directly in CRXDE:**

1. Open CRXDE → go to the **Query** tab (top menu)
2. Run this XPath query to find the node with the email:
   ```
   /jcr:root/content/emc/en-us/voucher/cdt-home/third-party-vouchers/jcr:content//*[jcr:contains(., 'ProvenHelpDeskVendoreMail')]
   ```
3. Click the result node — look for a property (`desc` or `text`) containing `ProvenHelpDeskVendoreMail@Dell.com`
4. Update both occurrences in the value:
   - `mailto:ProvenHelpDeskVendoreMail@Dell.com` → `mailto:services.learning.support@dell.com`
   - The display text `ProvenHelpDeskVendoreMail@Dell.com` → `services.learning.support@dell.com`
5. Click **Save All**
6. Replicate the page to publish

Since this is authored content and not in the codebase, there's no code change to commit here — the fix lives entirely in the JCR on the author instance.

Yes, in CRXDE open the **Query** tab:

1. At the top of CRXDE, click the **Query** (or **Tools > Query**) tab — it looks like a magnifying glass icon or is accessible via the toolbar
2. Set the type to **XPath**
3. Paste this in the query box:
   ```
   /jcr:root/content/emc/en-us/voucher/cdt-home/third-party-vouchers/jcr:content//*[jcr:contains(., 'ProvenHelpDeskVendoreMail')]
   ```
4. Click **Execute**

It will return the node(s) containing that email. Click the result path and it'll highlight the node in the left tree panel where you can edit the property.

The email is in that `href`. Now in CRXDE: 1. Click the result node that was returned by the query — it will highlight in the left tree 2. In the **Properties** panel (bottom right), find the property containing that HTML (likely `desc` or `text`) 3. Double-click the property value to edit it 4.
